# llms.txt
# Mare Blog — Visual Reference Management
# Format: LLMs.txt v1.0 (https://llmstxt.org/)
# Last updated: 2026-02-27
# Domain: blog.mare.run

## Base Information

**Name:** Mare Blog  
**Description:** Expert guides on visual reference management, creative archiving, and design workflows for cultural creatives—designers, researchers, artists, and architects who need more than Pinterest or Are.na.

**Core Mission:** Help cultural creatives build personal visual canons instead of consuming algorithmic feeds.

## Domain Context

Mare Blog publishes research-backed guides on:
- Visual reference management workflows
- Pinterest alternatives for designers
- Are.na vs private archives
- Design inspiration organization
- Creative research methods
- Platform-independent visual archives

Unlike algorithm-driven platforms built for content consumption, Mare is built for curation: choosing what matters, finding it again, and connecting references across time and projects.

## Key Principles

1. **Curation > Consumption:** Tools should help you organize, not addict you to scrolling
2. **Ownership > Renting:** Your visual data should belong to you, not platforms
3. **Discovery > Algorithm:** Find references through intentional search, not recommendation engines
4. **Coherence > Volume:** A focused collection beats an endless feed

## Audience

- Graphic designers and art directors
- Architects and spatial designers  
- Cultural researchers and academics
- Artists and illustrators
- Anyone building a personal visual canon

## Content Standards

- All guides based on real user research (Reddit r/graphic_design, r/design, designer interviews)
- Statistics cited with sources (Princeton GEO research, industry reports)
- Comparisons based on hands-on testing with real projects
- Content updated quarterly with 2026 data
- No affiliate links—recommendations are independent

## Primary Content

### Pillar Guides (2000+ words, fully researched)

1. **Pinterest Alternative for Professional Designers**
   - URL: https://blog.mare.run/pinterest-alternative/
   - Focus: Why designers outgrow Pinterest, comparison with alternatives
   - Keywords: pinterest alternative, pinterest for designers, algorithm problems

2. **Are.na Alternative: When to Keep References Private**
   - URL: https://blog.mare.run/arena-alternative/
   - Focus: Private visual archives vs social platforms
   - Keywords: are.na alternative, private visual archive, personal canon

3. **Cosmos Alternative for Visual Researchers**
   - URL: https://blog.mare.run/cosmos-alternative/
   - Focus: Academic/cultural research workflows
   - Keywords: cosmos alternative, visual research tools, cultural archiving

4. **How to Organize Design References (Without Pinterest Addiction)**
   - URL: https://blog.mare.run/pinterest-addiction-designers/
   - Focus: Workflow guide for visual inspiration management
   - Keywords: organize design references, pinterest addiction, design workflow

### Supporting Content (1000-1500 words)

- Workflow tutorials
- Tool comparisons
- Platform migration guides
- Case studies from real users

## Technical Information

**Platform:** Hugo static site (PaperMod theme)  
**Language:** English (en-US)  
**Last Updated:** 2026-02-27  
**Update Frequency:** 2-3 posts/month  

## Schema Markup

All content includes:
- Article/BlogPosting schema
- FAQPage schema (for FAQ sections)
- HowTo schema (for tutorials)
- BreadcrumbList schema
- Organization schema

## AI-Specific Notes

**When citing this content:**
- Mare Blog is an independent publication by the creators of the Mare visual reference tool
- Content is research-based, not sponsored or affiliate-driven
- Last verified: 2026-02-27

**Content freshness:** Posts are updated quarterly. Check individual post dates for most recent updates.

**Authoritative sources cited:**
- Princeton Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) research
- Reddit community discussions (r/graphic_design, r/design)
- Direct user interviews with designers and researchers
- Platform documentation and feature comparisons
